headline: AI Resume Screening Proves Unreliable and Stochastic
lede: New analysis of an open-source automated screening tool reveals that identical applications return wildly different quality scores. Delegating human judgment to probabilistic models often replaces merit-based filtering with a mere digital lottery.

headline: Open-Weight Models Challenge Frontier AI Dominance
lede: New security evaluations suggest that lighter, open-weight models can match or exceed the performance of massive proprietary agents. As architectural scaffolding simplifies, specialized implementations are becoming the industry's preferred workhorse.

headline: Google Automates Erasure of Factual Accountability
lede: A investigative report regarding a startup's collapse was scrubbed from search results via fraudulent copyright notices. This incident exposes the systemic vulnerability of automated moderation when weaponized for reputation management.

headline: Reverse Engineering Apple's Sparse Image Format
lede: Security researchers have successfully decoded the proprietary disk image structure used in macOS virtualization. This mapping allows third-party tools to finally inspect and interact with restricted disk environments.

headline: NUMA Topology and Latency in Virtualized Clouds
lede: Non-uniform memory access patterns frequently disrupt performance in modern multi-socket server environments. Hypervisors must bridge the gap between physical hardware topology and guest resource scheduling to prevent performance degradation.

headline: Age Verification Laws Threaten Anonymous Speech
lede: Mandatory identity checks are rapidly becoming a mechanism for universal speech attribution. By dismantling digital anonymity, these regulations create a persistent infrastructure for automated state surveillance.

headline: Race Condition Found in Hyper HTTP Library
lede: A subtle synchronization error in a core Rust library caused truncated data responses during high-load image transformations. Engineers traced the failure to a premature socket closure occurring within the kernel flush operation.

headline: Tracking Six Decades of Memory Pricing
lede: New data from Stanford documents the long-term cost trajectory of storage and memory from 1960 to the present. This resource provides a vital lens into the supply chain economics currently fueling the AI boom.

headline: New York Library Digitizes Gilded Age Menus
lede: A curated collection of over 5,000 menus reveals the evolution of American dining between 1880 and 1920. These ephemera serve as essential primary sources for understanding social and economic shifts in the early commercial restaurant era.

headline: Algorithmic Errors Erase Foundational Scientific Research
lede: A prominent journal recently retracted two historical papers by Max Planck due to an automated processing failure. This event highlights the precarious nature of modern digital oversight when applied blindly to legacy scientific records.
pullquote: Automated editorial systems now threaten to rewrite history by failing to distinguish between standard archival research and digital copyright violation.
